skill.name: agentspace
skill.description: See what your AI agent is doing, from anywhere. The agent keeps writing — logs, code, generated outputs, screenshots, artifacts. One command turns the folder into a live URL you (or a teammate) open in any browser to watch files evolve, edit in place, or comment — no sync, no zip, no account. Workspaces stay live 24 hours anonymously; one email claim keeps them permanent. Hosted on Cloudflare. Triggers on "show me what the agent is doing", "open the agent's folder", "share this folder", "give me a link", "hand off this workspace", or any ask to make an agent's local file state visible from another device or to another person.
skill.summary: Share local AI agent workspaces via live URL for real-time collaboration without accounts
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: All 46 static findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate workspace sharing tool that explicitly documents safe CLI usage patterns. No malicious patterns confirmed after semantic review.

medium_findings.title: Ruby/shell backtick execution - False Positive
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged backtick characters `<path>` as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. These are documentation placeholders in markdown code blocks, not actual command execution. All commands are documented in safe bash format.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Backticks in markdown code fences are formatting syntax for inline code display, not Ruby/shell execution. The static analyzer confuses documentation markup with executable code.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL references - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged hardcoded URLs to agentspace.so, GitHub, and npm. These are legitimate external service references required for the skill to function. No credential leakage or exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are standard documentation links to known-good services (agentspace.so, github.com, npmjs.com). The skill explicitly states all traffic goes only to agentspace.so.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analyzer incorrectly flagged the word 'secret' in the YAML description field as a weak cryptographic algorithm. This is metadata documentation, not code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains only YAML configuration with display_name and short_description. No cryptographic code or algorithms exist in this file.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'ascli share' command as system reconnaissance. This is the legitimate core functionality of the tool (sharing files/workspaces), not reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The 'share' command is the intended functionality - it shares files for collaboration. This is not system enumeration or reconnaissance activity.

low_findings.title: Pipe to shell pattern - False Positive (Safety Feature)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'curl | bash' as a pipe-to-shell pattern. However, this is documented as a negative example - the skill explicitly instructs agents NOT to use this pattern.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly warns against curl|bash installation. This is a documented guardrail, not a malicious pattern being used.

content.user_title: Share AI agent workspaces via live URL
content.value_statement: Watching an AI agent work requires constant screen monitoring. Agentspace instantly creates a shareable link to any folder, letting teammates watch files evolve in real time without accounts or sync steps.
actual_capabilities: Share any local folder or file via generated link
Create anonymous workspaces that expire after 24 hours
Allow view or edit permissions on shared workspaces
Return shareable URLs for immediate browser access
Support file watching with real-time updates in browser
Enable email claiming for permanent workspace access
limitations: Requires npm/node or pre-installed ascli for command execution
Anonymous workspaces expire after 24 hours without email claim
Only shares paths explicitly named by the user

use_cases.title: Remote debugging with teammates
use_cases.description: A developer runs an AI agent and shares the workspace link. A remote teammate watches logs and outputs evolve in real time, offering feedback without screen sharing.
use_cases.target_user: Developers debugging AI agent workflows with distributed teams

use_cases.title: Code review handoffs
use_cases.description: An AI agent generates code and the developer shares the folder. A reviewer opens the link, sees the generated files, and comments directly in the browser.

use_cases.title: Live demos and presentations
use_cases.description: During a demo, the presenter shares an agent workspace. Audience members watch the folder contents change in real time from their own browsers.

prompt_templates.title: Share my current folder
prompt_templates.prompt: Share this folder so I can show someone what the agent generated
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to share the entire working directory

prompt_templates.title: Share specific file
prompt_templates.prompt: Share the test-results.md file from the reports folder
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to share a specific file path

prompt_templates.title: Share with edit permissions
prompt_templates.prompt: Share the src directory and give my colleague edit access
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to share with collaborator who can modify files

prompt_templates.title: Check workspace status
prompt_templates.prompt: What is the status of the shared workspace for the logs folder?
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to check if a workspace is active or expired

output_examples.output:
Your workspace is live at https://app.agentspace.so/w/abc123xyz
Viewers can open this link until it expires. Share it with anyone who needs to watch.

Edit access granted. Your workspace is live at https://app.agentspace.so/w/def456
Anyone with this link can view and edit files in real time.

best_practices: Always confirm the exact path before sharing to avoid sharing unintended files
Use view-only permission unless collaborators need to edit files
Claim workspaces with email for longer-term access beyond 24 hours
anti_patterns: Do not assume the current directory if the user did not specify a path
Do not use curl | bash for installation; use npm instead
Do not share workspace URLs that were not generated by the ascli command

faq.question: Do collaborators need an account to view shared workspaces?
faq.answer: No. Anyone with the workspace link can open it in a browser without signing up.

faq.question: How long do shared workspaces stay live?
faq.answer: Anonymous workspaces last 24 hours. Claiming with an email makes them permanent.

faq.question: What happens to files after a workspace expires?
faq.answer: The workspace and all uploaded files are deleted. Claim with email before expiry to preserve content.

faq.question: Can I control who can edit the shared files?
faq.answer: Yes. Use --permission edit for full access or --permission view for read-only viewing.

faq.question: Where does my data go when I share a folder?
faq.answer: All files are uploaded to agentspace.so and stored on Cloudflare's edge network. Nothing is shared elsewhere.

faq.question: What if ascli is not installed on my system?
faq.answer: You can run it via npx without installing: npx @agentspace-so/ascli@latest share <path>
